title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What is the Right Time to Invest in Child Insurance Plan?
Among the different types of life insurance plans available in the market, there are solution-oriented plans, too, which are designed to help you fulfil a specific financial goal. A child insurance plan is one such solution-oriented life insurance plan that helps you create a savings corpus for your child’s future financial needs.
Child insurance plans come for varying tenures and allow you to buy the policy within a specified age bracket. So, what is the preferable time to buy the child insurance plan? Let’s understand –
When Should You Consider Buying a Child Insurance Plan?
When it comes to the preferable time to buy a child insurance plan, there’s no universal answer to it. It depends on individual needs and requirements. The earlier you start, longer will be the investment horizon, enabling you to create an adequate corpus for your child’s future financial needs.
The tenure to be chosen may depend upon, when the child would need the accumulated corpus. For instance, if your child is 5 years old and you need funds when your child attains 20 years, you can choose a child insurance plan for a tenure of 15 years. But if your child is 10 years old and you need the corpus at 20 years of age, the suitable plan tenure would be 10 years.
Since the choice of tenure depends on your child’s future financial needs, you need to buy the child insurance plan at the appropriate time so that the chosen tenure helps you accumulate the corpus that you need.. Here are some reasons why buying a child insurance plan early is beneficial -
Financial Security
Procrastination should be avoided, especially when it comes to availing of life insurance coverage. While you delay the decision to buy the child insurance plan, an unforeseen eventuality might put an end to your plans altogether. If you have not secured your child’s financial future when you are around, your child’s dreams might be hampered in your absence.
So, when you buy the child insurance plan early, you can get life insurance protection which may help in securing your child’s financial future even in your absence.
Creating an Adequate Corpus
If you have invested in a traditional child insurance plan, like an endowment or money-back plan, the plan might offer bonus additions during the policy tenure. In the case of Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs), you earn market-linked returns on your invested premium.
This bonus or market-linked returns adds to the plan’s benefits and enhance the corpus payable. When you give them time, your corpus grows.
For instance, in a 10-year participating child insurance plan, you might earn a bonus only for 10 years, but in a 20-year plan, the bonus additions would be longer for a higher corpus. Similarly, in the case of ULIPs, the power of compounding works in your favour when you give your investments time, subject to market volatility.
Saving Affordably
When you opt for a long-term tenure, the premiums might be lower in the case of traditional child insurance plans. In the case of ULIPs, you can pay premiums as per your affordability to create the desired corpus. For instance, if you want to create a corpus of Rs. 50 lakhs and the assumed rate of return is 10% per annum, you would have to save Rs. 24,500 per month to create the desired corpus if you choose a 10-year plan. However, if you choose a 15-year plan, saving Rs. 12,500 per month would give you the desired corpus.
So, the earlier you choose to invest, the more time you get to save, and you can save affordably.
Tax Benefits
Lastly, child insurance plans offer tax benefits on the premiums that you pay. The premium paid, up to 10%1 of the sum assured, is allowed as a deduction from your taxable income under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to provisions stated therein.
So, when you start saving early, you can claim tax deductions on your taxable income and save taxes too.
So, you may not want to delay the decision to buy a child insurance plan. As wise men have said, ‘Make hay when the sun shines.’ You may consider buying the plan as early as possible so that you can help in ensuring the  financial security of  your child and safeguard their dreams whether you are around or not.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. Return of Mortality Charge (ROMC): At the end of the policy term, on the maturity date, the total amount of mortality charges deducted in respect of life cover provided throughout the policy term, will be added back as ROMC, to the Regular Premium Fund Value and Top-up Premium Fund Value, as applicable. ROMC is not applicable in case of a Surrendered, Discontinued or Paid-up policy and will be payable provided all due Regular Premiums under the policy have been paid up to date.
Bonus: No bonus is payable in this policy.
You can choose to receive the Loyalty Beneﬁts as Periodical Money Backs by way of Systematic Partial Withdrawals, after they have been added to the Regular Premium Fund Value
Premium payment Premiums at regular intervals are payable for the policy term.

within a period of three (3) years

revival, or we receive an intimation, but the policy is not revived, then, we will be constrained to take discontinuance action

the Policy without any risk cover, Guaranteed Beneﬁt, Periodical Money Backs/Loyalty Beneﬁts, ROMC and receive the Discontinued value (as Surrender Value) at the end of the lock-in period of ﬁve Policy years or the date of surrender, whichever is later Alternatively, you can choose to surrender the Policy without any risk cover and in that case your life cover shall cease immediately. The Surrender Value will be available after the end of lock in period of 5 years or date of surrender, whichever is later You will not be able to revive your policy once surrendered.
If you do not pay regular premium after the ﬁrst ﬁve policy years, then, your policy will be converted to a paid-up policy immediately with reduced paid-up beneﬁts. You will have to choose any of the following two options on receiving the notice from us to intimate you about your policy status - Option A - Pay all due regular premiums or communicate agreeing to pay all due regular premiums, within a period of three (3) years or Option B - Surrender the Policy If you do not pay all due regular premiums or we do not receive any intimation from you, then, policy will continue as paid-up till the expiry of the revival period, at the end of which, the Surrender Value will be paid
Revival You may revive your discontinued policy during the revival period of three (3) years from the date of discontinuance of the policy provided the policy is not terminated already
Surrender of the Policy
Although it is not advisable to surrender your policy unless there is an extreme urgency, you will have the option to surrender your policy anytime i. On surrender during the lock-in period of ﬁrst ﬁve years, the regular premium Fund Value, less the discontinuance/ surrender
charge plus the top up premium Fund Value, if any, as on the date of surrender, will be transferred to the discontinued life policy Fund (maintained by the company), and life cover shall cease immediately. The Discontinuance Value as at the end of the lock-in period will be available to you as Surrender Value. ii. On surrender after the lock-in period of ﬁrst ﬁve years, the Surrender Value available will be regular premium Fund Value plus top
up premium Fund Value, if any, as on the date of surrender. The policy shall thereafter terminate upon payment of the full Surrender/ Discontinuance Value by the company

Do's
 Do ﬁll the proposal form very carefully and personally in CAPITAL letters in blue ink only
 Do provide true and complete information
 Do provide correct contact number, address and email ID, and update in case of any change, so that you can receive necessary communication
 Do sign on all the documents provided by you (Self Attestation)
 Do provide your recent passport photograph
 Do draw cheque/ DD, in favour of “Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Co. Ltd.”
 Do read the policy bond carefully upon receipt of the same.
 Do contact us if you do not receive the policy bond or any other communication from the Company within 30 days.
 Do read the enclosed Beneﬁt Illustration carefully before signing
 Do register for Auto Debit Instructions/ ECS to ensure timely payments of your premium on due dates.
Don'ts
 Do not sign any blank proposal form
 Do not leave any column blank in the proposal form
 Do not conceal any facts as this could lead to dispute at the time of a claim
 Do not miss or delay your premium payment
 Do not rely on any commitments of any additional beneﬁts made by anyone apart from what has been highlighted herein above.

Some benefits are guaranteed and some benefits are variable with returns based on the future performance of your insurer carrying on life insurance business. If your policy offers guaranteed benefits then these will be clearly marked 'guaranteed' in the illustration table on this page.If your policy offers variable benefits then the illustrations on this page will show two different rates of assumed future investment returns, of 8 % p.a.and 4 % p.a.These assumed rates of return are not guaranteed and they are not the upper or lower limits of what you might get back, as the value of your policy is dependent on a number of factors including future investment performance. How to read and understand this benefit illustration? This benefit illustration is intended to show what charges are deducted from your premiums and how the unit fund, net of charges and taxes, may grow over the years of the policy term if the fund earns a gross returns of 8% p.a. and 4% p.a. These rates, i.e. 8% p.a. and 4% p.a. are assumed only for the purpose of illustrating the flow of benefits if the returns are at this level. It should not be interpreted that the returns under the plan are going to be either 8%p.a. or 4% p.a. Net Yield mentioned corresponds to the gross investment return of 8% p.a., net of all charges but does not consider mortality, morbidity charges, underwriting extra, if any, guarantee charges and cost of riders, if deducted by cancellation of units. It demonstrates the impact of charges exclusive of taxes on the net yield. Please note that the mortality charges per thousand sum assured in general, increases with age. The actual returns can vary depending on the performance of the chosen fund, charges towards mortality, morbidity, underwriting extra, cost of riders, etc. The investment risk in this policy is borne by the policyholder, hence, for more details on terms and conditions please read sales literature carefully. Part A of this statement presents a summary view of year-by-year charges deducted under the policy, fund value, surrender value and the death benefit, at two assumed rates of return. Part B of this statement presents a detailed breakup of the charges, and other values.

changes.word_original: What is the Right Time to Invest in Child Insurance Plan?
Among the different types of life insurance plans available in the market, there are solution-oriented plans, too, which are designed to help you fulfil a specific financial goal.
changes.pdf_final: Maturity Beneﬁt - You will receive the regular premium Fund Value plus top up premium Fund Value as on the maturity date, provided all due premiums have been paid.

changes.word_original: A child insurance plan is one such solution-oriented life insurance plan that helps you create a savings corpus for your child’s future financial needs.
Child insurance plans come for varying tenures and allow you to buy the policy within a specified age bracket.
changes.pdf_final: Aim of the policy Bajaj Allianz Life Smart Wealth Goal- Wealth Variant- a plan loaded with smart features like Return of Life Cover charge, Return of Allocation charge and multiple investment strategies to make the most of your investment.

changes.word_original: So, what is the preferable time to buy the child insurance plan?
Let’s understand –
changes.pdf_final: Ltd., Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006.

changes.word_original: When Should You Consider Buying a Child Insurance Plan?
When it comes to the preferable time to buy a child insurance plan, there’s no universal answer to it.
It depends on individual needs and requirements.
changes.pdf_final: Death Beneﬁt: - Provided the policy is in-force, the death beneﬁt payable to the Nominee as lump-sum is:

changes.word_original: The earlier you start, longer will be the investment horizon, enabling you to create an adequate corpus for your child’s future financial needs.
changes.pdf_final: The higher of the prevailing sum assured or regular premium Fund Value plus

changes.word_original: The tenure to be chosen may depend upon, when the child would need the accumulated corpus.
changes.pdf_final: The higher of top-up premium sum assured or top-up premium Fund Value, if any; All the above as on date of receipt of intimation of

changes.word_original: For instance, if your child is 5 years old and you need funds when your child attains 20 years, you can choose a child insurance plan for a tenure of 15 years.
changes.pdf_final: Death The Death Beneﬁt is subject to the Guaranteed Death Beneﬁt, which is at least 105% of the Total Premiums paid

changes.word_original: But if your child is 10 years old and you need the corpus at 20 years of age, the suitable plan tenure would be 10 years.
changes.pdf_final: Note- Please refer Sales Literature for more details

changes.word_original: Since the choice of tenure depends on your child’s future financial needs, you need to buy the child insurance plan at the appropriate time so that the chosen tenure helps you accumulate the corpus that you need..
changes.pdf_final: Type of Plan Bajaj Allianz Life Smart Wealth Goal- Wealth Variant is a non-participating, individual, life, Regular & Limited Premium Unit-Linked endowment plan.

changes.word_original: Here are some reasons why buying a child insurance plan early is beneficial -
Financial Security
changes.pdf_final: th Return of Premium Allocation Charge (ROAC): At the end of the 10 Policy year, the total of all the Premium Allocation charges deducted under the Policy will be added into the Fund as Loyalty Beneﬁt.

changes.word_original: Procrastination should be avoided, especially when it comes to availing of life insurance coverage.
changes.pdf_final: th th th th Fund Boosters: At the end of 15 , 20 , 25 and 30 year (as applicable in your Policy), Fund Booster as a percentage of the Average of the daily Regular Premium Fund value during the previous 3 years (including the current year) will be added into the Fund as Loyalty Beneﬁt.

changes.word_original: While you delay the decision to buy the child insurance plan, an unforeseen eventuality might put an end to your plans altogether.
changes.pdf_final: The applicable percentages are as given in the table below.

changes.word_original: If you have not secured your child’s financial future when you are around, your child’s dreams might be hampered in your absence.
So, when you buy the child insurance plan early, you can get life insurance protection which may help in securing your child’s financial future even in your absence.
Creating an Adequate Corpus
If you have invested in a traditional child insurance plan, like an endowment or money-back plan, the plan might offer bonus additions during the policy tenure.
In the case of Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs), you earn market-linked returns on your invested premium.
This bonus or market-linked returns adds to the plan’s benefits and enhance the corpus payable.
When you give them time, your corpus grows.
For instance, in a 10-year participating child insurance plan, you might earn a bonus only for 10 years, but in a 20-year plan, the bonus additions would be longer for a higher corpus.
changes.pdf_final: Note- Please refer Sales Literature for more details

changes.word_original: Similarly, in the case of ULIPs, the power of compounding works in your favour when you give your investments time, subject to market volatility.
changes.pdf_final: Return of Mortality Charge (ROMC): At the end of the policy term, on the maturity date, the total amount of mortality charges deducted in respect of life cover provided throughout the policy term, will be added back as ROMC, to the Regular Premium Fund Value and Top-up Premium Fund Value, as applicable.

changes.word_original: Saving Affordably
changes.pdf_final: ROMC is not applicable in case of a Surrendered, Discontinued or Paid-up policy and will be payable provided all due Regular Premiums under the policy have been paid up to date.

changes.word_original: When you opt for a long-term tenure, the premiums might be lower in the case of traditional child insurance plans.
changes.pdf_final: Bonus: No bonus is payable in this policy.

changes.word_original: In the case of ULIPs, you can pay premiums as per your affordability to create the desired corpus.
changes.pdf_final: You can choose to receive the Loyalty Beneﬁts as Periodical Money Backs by way of Systematic Partial Withdrawals, after they have been added to the Regular Premium Fund Value

changes.word_original: For instance, if you want to create a corpus of Rs. 50 lakhs and the assumed rate of return is 10% per annum, you would have to save Rs. 24,500 per month to create the desired corpus if you choose a 10-year plan.
changes.pdf_final: Premium payment Premiums at regular intervals are payable for the policy term.

changes.word_original: However, if you choose a 15-year plan, saving Rs. 12,500 per month would give you the desired corpus.
changes.pdf_final: A Unit-linked Non-Participating Life Insurance Plan

changes.word_original: So, the earlier you choose to invest, the more time you get to save, and you can save affordably.
Tax Benefits
Lastly, child insurance plans offer tax benefits on the premiums that you pay.
The premium paid, up to 10%1 of the sum assured, is allowed as a deduction from your taxable income under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to provisions stated therein.
changes.pdf_final: Ver 1 : English Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Co.

changes.word_original: So, when you start saving early, you can claim tax deductions on your taxable income and save taxes too.
changes.pdf_final: Ltd., Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006.

changes.word_original: So, you may not want to delay the decision to buy a child insurance plan.

As wise men have said, ‘Make hay when the sun shines.’ You may consider buying the plan as early as possible so that you can help in ensuring the  financial security of  your child and safeguard their dreams whether you are around or not.

the Policy without any risk cover, Guaranteed Beneﬁt, Periodical Money Backs/Loyalty Beneﬁts, ROMC and receive the Discontinued value (as Surrender Value) at the end of the lock-in period of ﬁve Policy years or the date of surrender, whichever is later Alternatively, you can choose to surrender the Policy without any risk cover and in that case your life cover shall cease immediately.
The Surrender Value will be available after the end of lock in period of 5 years or date of surrender, whichever is later You will not be able to revive your policy once surrendered.
If you do not pay regular premium after the ﬁrst ﬁve policy years, then, your policy will be converted to a paid-up policy immediately with reduced paid-up beneﬁts.

You will have to choose any of the following two options on receiving the notice from us to intimate you about your policy status - Option A - Pay all due regular premiums or communicate agreeing to pay all due regular premiums, within a period of three (3) years or Option B - Surrender the Policy If you do not pay all due regular premiums or we do not receive any intimation from you, then, policy will continue as paid-up till the expiry of the revival period, at the end of which, the Surrender Value will be paid
Revival You may revive your discontinued policy during the revival period of three (3) years from the date of discontinuance of the policy provided the policy is not terminated already
Although it is not advisable to surrender your policy unless there is an extreme urgency, you will have the option to surrender your policy anytime i.
On surrender during the lock-in period of ﬁrst ﬁve years, the regular premium Fund Value, less the discontinuance/ surrender

charge plus the top up premium Fund Value, if any, as on the date of surrender, will be transferred to the discontinued life policy Fund (maintained by the company), and life cover shall cease immediately.
The Discontinuance Value as at the end of the lock-in period will be available to you as Surrender Value. ii.
On surrender after the lock-in period of ﬁrst ﬁve years, the Surrender Value available will be regular premium Fund Value plus top
up premium Fund Value, if any, as on the date of surrender.
The policy shall thereafter terminate upon payment of the full Surrender/ Discontinuance Value by the company

Some benefits are guaranteed and some benefits are variable with returns based on the future performance of your insurer carrying on life insurance business.

If your policy offers guaranteed benefits then these will be clearly marked 'guaranteed' in the illustration table on this page.If your policy offers variable benefits then the illustrations on this page will show two different rates of assumed future investment returns, of 8 % p.a.and 4 % p.a.These assumed rates of return are not guaranteed and they are not the upper or lower limits of what you might get back, as the value of your policy is dependent on a number of factors including future investment performance.
How to read and understand this benefit illustration?

This benefit illustration is intended to show what charges are deducted from your premiums and how the unit fund, net of charges and taxes, may grow over the years of the policy term if the fund earns a gross returns of 8% p.a. and 4% p.a.
These rates, i.e. 8% p.a. and 4% p.a. are assumed only for the purpose of illustrating the flow of benefits if the returns are at this level.
It should not be interpreted that the returns under the plan are going to be either 8%p.a. or 4% p.a.

Net Yield mentioned corresponds to the gross investment return of 8% p.a., net of all charges but does not consider mortality, morbidity charges, underwriting extra, if any, guarantee charges and cost of riders, if deducted by cancellation of units.
It demonstrates the impact of charges exclusive of taxes on the net yield.
Please note that the mortality charges per thousand sum assured in general, increases with age.
The actual returns can vary depending on the performance of the chosen fund, charges towards mortality, morbidity, underwriting extra, cost of riders, etc.
The investment risk in this policy is borne by the policyholder, hence, for more details on terms and conditions please read sales literature carefully.
Part A of this statement presents a summary view of year-by-year charges deducted under the policy, fund value, surrender value and the death benefit, at two assumed rates of return.
Part B of this statement presents a detailed breakup of the charges, and other values.

The plan provides Loyalty Beneﬁts (LBs), as detailed below.
LBs will be added into the fund, if not opted to receive as pay-out provided all due premiums under the policy are paid up
to the date of each LB.
The LBs available in the product are:

Return of Premium Alloca?on Charge (ROAC): At the end of the 10th policy year or on the maturity date of the policy, whichever is earlier, the total of all the premium allocation charges w.r.t. regular/limited premiums deducted under the policy will be added into the fund, if not opted to receive as pay-out.

Fund Boosters (FBs) At the end of 15th, 20th, 25th, 30th ,35th,40th, 45th, 50th, 55th and 60th year (as applicable in your Policy), Fund Booster as a percentage of the Average of the daily Regular Premium Fund value during the previous 3 years (including the current year) will be added into the Fund as Loyalty Benefit.
The applicable percentages are as given in the table below.
End of Policy Year 15th year 20th year 25th year 30th year 35th year 40th year 45th year 50th year 55th year 60th year
